gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Fallibility classification for an exported op."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Fallibility { # [doc = " The op is total: it cannot fail for any well-formed input."] Total , # [doc = " The op can fail; the error set is described in `error_set`."] Fallible , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether an op has an EXPLAIN obligation (C3)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Explainable { # [doc = " The op selects / converts / approximates and must expose a reified artifact."] Yes , # [doc = " The op reports a deterministic fact; there is no hidden decision to explain (C3 `n/a`)."] NotApplicable , }
gaps.reason: dropped non-doc attribute(s) on enum `Explainable`: # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)]

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One row in the guarantee matrix (RFC-0016 §4.5)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct MatrixRow { # [doc = " The exported operation name."] pub op : & 'static str , # [doc = " Guarantee tag on `Exact ⊐ Proven ⊐ Empirical ⊐ Declared` (VR-5)."] # [doc = " All rows are `\"Exact\"` because content hashing is deterministic (spec §4)."] pub guarantee : & 'static str , # [doc = " Whether the op can fail and what the explicit error set is."] pub fallibility : Fallibility , # [doc = " The explicit error / none-case description (empty string for total ops)."] pub error_set : & 'static str , # [doc = " Declared effects (C6). Empty string means no effects."] pub effects : & 'static str , # [doc = " Whether the op has a C3 EXPLAIN obligation."] pub explainable : Explainable , }
gaps.reason: struct `MatrixRow` uses named fields — no record/product-type surface (only a single-ctor positional shape maps to `type_item`)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `std.content` guarantee matrix.  One row per exported op, encoded as data and asserted in"] # [doc = " `tests` — never prose-only (RFC-0016 §4.5; spec §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **All rows are `Exact`** (deterministic): a content hash is a pure function of normalized"] # [doc = " structure (RFC-0001 §4.6 WF4; ADR-003). **All rows declare no effects** (C6: pure reads)."] # [doc = " **All rows are `NotApplicable` for EXPLAIN** (C3: no selection/conversion/approximation)."] pub const MATRIX : & [MatrixRow] = & [MatrixRow { op : "hash_of_value" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Total , error_set : "" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "hash_of_def" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Total , error_set : "" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "digest_eq" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Total , error_set : "" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "as_ref" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Total , error_set : "" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "parse_ref" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Fallible , error_set : "Err(MalformedDigest) on bad shape" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "resolve_name" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Fallible , error_set : "None when name unbound" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } , MatrixRow { op : "names_of" , guarantee : "Exact" , fallibility : Fallibility :: Total , error_set : "" , effects : "none" , explainable : Explainable :: NotApplicable , } ,] ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const MATRIX` — no const item production in the grammar (`item` covers use/default/type/trait/impl/fn/object/lower/derive only)

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: { Explainable , Fallibility , MatrixRow , MATRIX } ; # [doc = " Every exported op named in the spec §3 surface appears in the matrix."] # [doc = " Guard: removing any op from MATRIX makes this fail."] # [test] fn matrix_contains_all_seven_exported_ops () { let expected = ["hash_of_value" , "hash_of_def" , "digest_eq" , "as_ref" , "parse_ref" , "resolve_name" , "names_of" ,] ; for name in & expected { assert ! (MATRIX . iter () . any (| r | r . op == * name) , "matrix is missing op {:?} (spec §3)" , name) ; } assert_eq ! (MATRIX . len () , expected . len () , "matrix has unexpected extra rows") ; } # [doc = " Every row carries the `Exact` guarantee tag (spec §4 / VR-5)."] # [doc = " Guard: changing any tag to a weaker label makes this fail."] # [test] fn every_row_is_exact () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . guarantee , "Exact" , "op {:?} must be Exact — content hashing is deterministic (RFC-0001 §4.6 WF4)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " Total ops have an empty error set; fallible ops have a non-empty one (C1)."] # [doc = " Guard: setting a fallible op's error_set to \"\" makes this fail."] # [test] fn fallibility_and_error_set_are_consistent () { for row in MATRIX { match row . fallibility { Fallibility :: Total => assert ! (row . error_set . is_empty () , "total op {:?} must have an empty error_set" , row . op) , Fallibility :: Fallible => assert ! (! row . error_set . is_empty () , "fallible op {:?} must name its error set (C1)" , row . op) , } } } # [doc = " Every row declares no effects (C6)."] # [doc = " Guard: adding an effect to any row makes this fail."] # [test] fn every_row_declares_no_effects () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . effects , "none" , "op {:?} declares unexpected effects (C6): {:?}" , row . op , row . effects) ; } } # [doc = " Every row has EXPLAIN = NotApplicable (spec §4 / C3 n/a)."] # [doc = " Guard: marking any row Yes makes this fail."] # [test] fn every_row_is_not_applicable_for_explain () { for row in MATRIX { assert_eq ! (row . explainable , Explainable :: NotApplicable , "op {:?} unexpectedly requires EXPLAIN (C3 is n/a for std.content — spec §5/C3)" , row . op) ; } } # [doc = " The two fallible ops in the spec are `parse_ref` and `resolve_name`."] # [doc = " Guard: changing either op's fallibility makes this fail."] # [test] fn exactly_two_fallible_ops () { let fallible : Vec < & MatrixRow > = MATRIX . iter () . filter (| r | r . fallibility == Fallibility :: Fallible) . collect () ; assert_eq ! (fallible . len () , 2 , "expected exactly 2 fallible ops") ; assert ! (fallible . iter () . any (| r | r . op == "parse_ref")) ; assert ! (fallible . iter () . any (| r | r . op == "resolve_name")) ; } }
